patches.find: not independent predictions. No derivation connects
patches.replace: not independent predictions. Both NOAA's WMM2025 secular-variation forecast and the dome's purported exponential trajectory are fits to the same public NOAA NP.xy time series; they have not been shown to make distinguishable predictions at any future epoch. No derivation connects
patches.rationale: EXP-291: Insert one sentence after 'not independent predictions.' to preempt the surviving advocate defense (defense_survives=3, WIN-036.c3) that the dome's exponential trajectory was fit independently before WMM2025 publication. Neutralizes the 'independent trajectory' prong by showing both forecasts are common-source fits to the same NOAA NP.xy record with no demonstrated discrimination at any future epoch.

patches.find: the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.
patches.replace: for WIN-018 specifically the dome's weaker test cannot apply at all — monitor.py contains no analemma domain, no fetch, no comparison; the value 6.9 lives only as static prose in raw-text/02-wins.txt, instrumented nowhere. WIN-018 falls below even the static-win bar that Part 3.1b's smoking-gun argument addresses.
patches.rationale: ISS-1873: Tighten WIN-018 detail_verdict_text paragraph. The current text imports a 'hardcoded expected value sits in same commit as live fetch call' framing from Part 3.1b but WIN-018 has no fetch call and no hardcoded expected/observed pair in monitor.py. Replace with WIN-018-specific text: the value has zero monitor.py presence, falling below even the static-win bar.

patches.find: cherry-picking a derived sub-statistic — and the dome provides
patches.replace: cherry-picking a derived sub-statistic. The WIN label reads simply "RMS," without specifying obliquity-only sub-component — implying the published full equation-of-time RMS (8.8 min). The undisclosed substitution of a sub-statistic for the headline figure is what makes this cherry-picking rather than transparent disclosure — and the dome provides
patches.rationale: ISS-1874: Add advocate preempt sentence after cherry-picking accusation. Without it, a dome defender can argue '6.9 is the obliquity sub-component RMS which is precise, not cherry-picked.' The new sentence explains why it IS cherry-picking: the WIN label implies the headline full-EoT RMS without disclosing the sub-component substitution.
